p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Stable leaf density; yellow pot orientation consistent. Foliage remains turgid.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained steady state over the 5-day period. No significant growth or decline observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: Alignment: High. Leaf turgidity indicates successful moisture uptake and stable VPD management.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Two primary leaves present; central growth point visible. Located in the shared black pot.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Post-T-5, the white granular material (user-added) has remained stable on the soil surface. No further wilting observed since the initial stress event.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Recovery: Moderate. The cessation of leaf margin degradation suggests the user's intervention (likely soil amendment) has stabilized the root zone.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves present; white rabbit anchor (5cm) remains in position.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The leaf tip necrosis noted previously has not progressed. The plant remains in a static, stable state.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Alignment: Stable. The lack of further necrosis indicates the current humidity/moisture levels are sufficient to prevent further tissue damage.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen, located near the rim of the shared black pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remains dormant/static. No new leaf development observed over the 5-day sequence.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Alignment: Stable. No signs of chlorosis or desiccation; growth is currently in a slow-phase.

biome_observations.soil_surface: White granular material (user-added) is present in p2/p4 pot, confirming successful user intervention. Soil texture appears consistent.
biome_observations.desk_surface: No debris or anomalies detected. Lighting remains consistent with fixed LED/North window diffuse setup.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a transition from a stressed state (T-5) to a stabilized state (Current). The white granular material added at T-5 has successfully integrated into the soil surface without causing negative physiological reactions.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a 'Stabilized Recovery' phase. The primary stressor (VPD) appears to be under control as evidenced by the lack of further tissue necrosis across all specimens.
vision_report.anomalies: None. The white material on the soil is confirmed as a successful user-led care action.
vision_report.narrative_description: The garden has moved from a period of acute stress (T-5) to a period of maintenance and stabilization. The user's intervention with soil amendments has effectively halted the progression of dehydration in p2. All plants are currently in a state of equilibrium.
